course: CMU 21-254: Linear Algebra and Vector Calculus for Engineers
audience: SI leader teaching live on a whiteboard. This is not a student worksheet.
toneRule: Keep explanations very simple, verbal, and board-friendly. Avoid textbook phrasing unless the problem needs it.
title: Exam 3 Review
sessionGoal: Students should review curves, scalar functions, gradients, optimization, vector fields, line integrals, and conservative fields.
quickStartIfUnprepared: Start by saying: Exam 3 is curves + functions + vector fields.
Do Problems 2 and 3 for scalar functions and optimization.
Do Problems 4 and 5 for vector fields and line integrals.
sayThisFirst: For curves, think: position, velocity, speed.
For scalar functions, think: partial derivatives, gradient, tangent plane.
For optimization, set first partials equal to zero and classify.
For vector fields, decide whether the problem asks for divergence, curl, or a line integral.
For conservative fields, find a potential and use endpoints.
boardPlan: Write a two-column topic map: scalar functions vs vector fields.
Finish with one div/curl problem and one conservative line integral.
keyFormulas: \(D_uf=\nabla f\cdot u\), where \(u\) is a unit vector.
commonMistakes: Not unitizing the direction vector for directional derivatives.
Forgetting the endpoint order in the Fundamental Theorem of Line Integrals.
Using tangent plane formula without plugging in the base point.
notationWarnings: Use \(r'(t)\) for velocity and \(\|r'(t)\|\) for speed.
Use \(u\) for a unit direction vector.
For conservative fields, use a potential \(\phi\) to avoid confusing it with the vector field \(F\).

problems.title: Curve speed and arc length
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(r(t)=(2\cos t,2\sin t,t)\), \(0\le t\le\pi\). Find the speed and arc length.
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: differentiate first, then take the length of the derivative.

drawInstructions: Draw a half-turn helix around the z-axis.
Label radius 2 and upward motion z=t.
Draw tangent arrows and label speed sqrt(5).

commonMistakes: Taking the length of \(r(t)\) instead of \(r'(t)\).

problems.title: Directional derivative and tangent plane
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(f(x,y)=xe^y+y^2\). At \((1,0)\), find the directional derivative in the direction from \((1,0)\) toward \((4,4)\). Then find the tangent plane to \(z=f(x,y)\) at \((1,0)\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: first make the direction into a unit vector. Then use the same partials for the tangent plane.

drawInstructions: Draw the base point (1,0) in the xy-plane.
Draw direction vector from (1,0) toward (4,4), then label the unit vector (3/5,4/5).
Draw a tangent plane at height z=1.

walkthrough.label: Make the unit direction
walkthrough.latex: The direction from \((1,0)\) to \((4,4)\) is \((3,4)\), so the unit vector is \[u=\left(\frac35,\frac45\right).\]

commonMistakes: Using the direction point \((4,4)\) itself as the direction vector.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 7 for gradients and tangent planes.

problems.title: Critical point classification
problems.studentPromptLatex: Find and classify the critical point of \(f(x,y)=x^2-2xy+2y^2-4y\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: critical point first, then Hessian test second.

drawInstructions: Draw bowl-shaped level curves centered near (2,2).
Write D=4>0 and f_xx=2>0, so local minimum.

walkthrough.label: Second derivative test
walkthrough.latex: \[f_{xx}=2,\qquad f_{yy}=4,\qquad f_{xy}=-2.\] \[D=f_{xx}f_{yy}-(f_{xy})^2=2(4)-(-2)^2=4.\] Since \(D>0\) and \(f_{xx}>0\), this is a local minimum.

commonMistakes: Forgetting that \(D>0\) still needs \(f_{xx}\) to decide min vs max.

problems.title: Divergence and curl checkpoint
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(F(x,y)=\langle x^2-y,xy\rangle\). Find \(\operatorname{div}F\) and scalar curl \(Q_x-P_y\), then evaluate both at \((2,-1)\).
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: name P and Q first. That prevents most mistakes.

drawInstructions: Show div as P_x+Q_y and curl as Q_x-P_y.

problems.title: Conservative line integral
problems.studentPromptLatex: Let \(F(x,y)=\langle 2xy+1,x^2+3y^2\rangle\). Compute \(\int_C F\cdot dr\) from \((1,0)\) to \((2,1)\), assuming \(C\) is any smooth path between the points.
problems.whatToSayBefore: Say: because it says any smooth path, we should check whether the field is conservative.

drawInstructions: Draw multiple possible paths from (1,0) to (2,1).
Label them all with the same value because the field is conservative.

walkthrough.label: Check conservative
walkthrough.latex: Here \(P=2xy+1\) and \(Q=x^2+3y^2\). \[P_y=2x,\qquad Q_x=2x.\] So the field is conservative.

walkthrough.label: Find potential
walkthrough.latex: Integrate \(P\) with respect to \(x\): \[\phi=x^2y+x+g(y).\] Then \[\phi_y=x^2+g'(y)=x^2+3y^2,\] so \(g'(y)=3y^2\), meaning \(g(y)=y^3\).

commonMistakes: Trying to parametrize the path even though the field is conservative.

practiceLabPointer.note: Practice Lab Section 8 for path independence.

wrapUpQuestions: What are the big categories of Exam 3 problems?
Which formulas require a unit direction vector?
How do you know whether a line integral can use endpoints only?
qualityControlNotes: Checked helix speed sqrt(5) and length pi sqrt(5).
Checked gradient (1,1), directional derivative 7/5, tangent plane z=x+y.
Checked optimization critical point (2,2), value -4, local minimum.
Checked conservative potential phi=x^2y+x+y^3 and integral 6.
reviewSummary: Session 20 is an Exam 3 review set with five checked mixed problems.
